sql-02.output: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE country = 'Canada';

sql-03.output: SELECT SUM(total) FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed';

sql-04.output: SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY status;

sql-05.output: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.qty * p.price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY revenue ASC LIMIT 2;
sql-05.analysis: Sorted ascending instead of descending, so it returned the two *lowest*-revenue categories (Stationery, Furniture) rather than the top two. The join and aggregation were correct — only the ORDER BY direction was wrong, a subtle slip that deterministic execution catches immediately.

sql-06.output: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM orders);
